meta_title: For Students: deployment workflow to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Student Guide: Deployment Workflow to Solve 'No Portfolio'
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about no portfolio. Learn how to master a professional deployment workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first SaaS project.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by demonstrating production-grade delivery, not just code. By integrating Antigravity for infrastructure and Claude Code for rapid iteration, you create a live, scalable application. This workflow proves you can ship, maintain, and scale software—the exact skills hiring managers and venture investors demand.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a GitHub repository is the silent killer of student careers. You have the logic, the frameworks, and the vision, but your lack of a production-ready portfolio makes you invisible to recruiters. The industry doesn't care about your local `localhost:3000` environment; they care about what you can ship to the cloud.

Today, we bridge that gap. We are transforming your code from a collection of files into a living, breathing SaaS product. By mastering a professional deployment workflow, you stop being a student who writes code and start being an engineer who builds systems.

steps.title: Define Infrastructure-as-Code
steps.description: Before writing logic, define your cloud architecture. This ensures your app is reproducible and professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal Terraform or Pulumi configuration for a scalable Next.js application hosted on AWS, including basic VPC, S3 bucket for assets, and a CloudFront distribution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate IaC files for AWS deployment based on current architecture'

steps.title: Establish CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Automation is the mark of a pro. We set up an automated pipeline that tests and deploys on every push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and deploys the build to an S3 bucket upon merging to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create .github/workflows/deploy.yml with linting and S3 sync steps'

steps.title: Implement Database Migrations
steps.description: Never manually edit a database in production. Use versioned migrations to keep your schema consistent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema and migration strategy that supports user authentication and a basic content management system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize prisma schema and generate migration script for user model'

steps.title: Configure Monitoring and Logs
steps.description: A deployed app is useless if you can't see why it crashes. Add error tracking early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate Sentry error monitoring into the existing Next.js application and define a custom health check endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add sentry integration and configure health check route'

steps.title: The Final Production Deploy
steps.description: Perform a dry-run deployment to verify your workflow works end-to-end.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the entire deployment workflow for security gaps and suggest optimizations for faster build times.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute build and verify production environment variables'

pro_tips: Always use Environment Variables for secrets; never commit keys to your repo, even if it's private.
Tag your releases in Git; it shows potential employers that you understand professional version control cycles.
Add a 'status' badge to your README that links to your live deployment to prove the app is currently running.
Keep your deployment environment identical to your local dev environment using Docker containers.
Write a 'Post-Mortem' document in your repo for any bugs encountered during deployment; it shows high-level problem-solving maturity.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, update your LinkedIn and GitHub bio with the production URL, write a 300-word case study on the architectural challenges you solved, and start iterating on a second feature.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a developer who ships. Stop waiting for permission to build and start demonstrating your value to the world. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
